issue_title: Loading an older model with a custom operator from Java/Scala more than twice causes MXNet to crash
issue_body:
## Version
MXNet 1.1.0 with Scala

## Description
Our Scala application uses MxNet and includes several tests. Many of these tests load the same model files, so as a result the same process ends up loading a model multiple times. This normally works fine, but when upgrading from 0.11.0 to 1.1.0 we discovered an odd issue causing our tests to fail. We discovered that if you have a model that uses a custom operator and was built from an mxnet version older than the current one (thus prompting the "Attempting to upgrade..." message), and you load it more than twice in the same process, then on the third reload it crashes.

## Analysis
Based on my investigation, the relevant lines appear to be:

- `legacy_json_util.cc#UpgradeJSON_FixParsing (line 30)`: This method is run whenever a model older than the current version is loaded. It invokes the attribute parser for each op.
- `legacy_json_util.cc#UpgradeJSON_Parse (line 92)`: This method is always run. It also invokes the attribute parser for each op
- `custom.cc#AttrParser (line 80)`: This line resets the shared pointer to the custom operator's callback list. Note that it also sets up a deleter function that unregisters the op by invoking the function in the next bullet.
- `ml_dmlc_mxnet_native_c_api.cc#opPropDel (line 2426)`: This function unregisters the custom operator. However, there is logic here that tracks how many times this function has been invoked. It only deregisters the custom op on the _third_ invocation.

Suppose we are running MXNet 1.1.0 and load a model built on 0.11.0. Because of the version mismatch, both of the UpgradeJSON stages are run, each one running the attribute parser for each op. When the custom op's attribute parser is run the second time, the shared pointer to the operator's callback list is reset, triggering the unregister function to be invoked. This function, for now, simply updates a counter. The counter now has value 1.

Now suppose during the same process, we load the same model again. The same scenario as above happens again, except this time the counter is incremented to 2.

The third time the model is loaded, again the same scenario reoccurs, except this time the counter is already at value 2, so the unregister function actually unregisters the custom op rather than simply updating the counter. Our custom operator is no longer registered, so shortly thereafter we get an "Operator [op-name] is not registered" fatal error.

issue_title: Autograd fails when using `take` operator repeatedly
issue_body:
## Description
Here provides a strange example that a backward pass on the accumulated sum of a 2-d NDArray `sc`, may potentially cause autograd to fail, depending on the shape of `sc`.

## Minimum reproducible example

TL;DR. This code calculates `x = sum(sc[i, j] for each i, j)`, and then invokes a backward pass `x.backward()`. The error message says that it could not calculate the gradient w.r.t. some variables, but I didn't require gradient for non-differentiable variables like `i` or `j`.

```python
import mxnet as mx

def _array(shape):
    """Create an NDArray with random entries and the given shape
    """
    return mx.nd.random.uniform(-1.0, 1.0, shape=shape, dtype="float32")

def index(sc, i, j):
    """Equivalent to sc[i, j] in numpy
    """
    return sc.take(i).squeeze(axis=0)  \
             .take(j).squeeze(axis=0)

# tweaking `sc` in different shapes, the code sometimes fails, sometimes not
row_len, col_len = 2, 8

# the scanned variable
sc = _array((row_len, col_len))
sc.attach_grad()  # we only require the gradient w.r.t. `sc`

# `i`, `j` are loop variables which don't require grad
i = mx.nd.array([0], dtype="int64")
j = mx.nd.array([0], dtype="int64")

with mx.autograd.record(train_mode=True):
    xs = []
    for _ in range(row_len):
        x_i = []
        for _ in range(col_len):
            x_ij = index(sc, i, j)
            x_i.append(x_ij)
            j = j + 1
        i = i + 1
        j = j - col_len  # reset j
        xs.append(mx.nd.stack(*x_i))
    x = mx.nd.stack(*xs)
    x = x.sum()

x.backward()
print(sc.grad.asnumpy())   # the expected result should be all `1`s
```

I am trying to implement a custom convolution operation, following the [Gluon tutorial](http://gluon.mxnet.io/chapter04_convolutional-neural-networks/cnn-scratch.html), however I cannot make it work without bias term.

```Python
import mxnet as mx

xx = nd.random_uniform(shape=[nbatch,nchannels,5,5])
weight = nd.ones(shape=[nfilters,nchannels,kernel[0],kernel[1]])
b1 = nd.zeros(shape=[nfilters,])

conv = nd.Convolution(data=xx,weight=weight,bias=b1,
```

issue_title: timeout and connect_timeout are not being passed to the FidoClient
issue_body:
The call to [fido.fetch(....)](https://github.com/Yelp/bravado/blob/3c00a50f8a7d634cf179d771d371a200a22c0df6/bravado/fido_client.py#L68) doesn't currently pass along the `timeout` kwarg. 

After https://github.com/Yelp/fido/issues/12 is fixed, it should also pass along the ```connect_timeout``` kwarg too.

This is not to be confused with the ```timeout``` passed to ```future.result(timeout)``` since time may have passed between to call to ```fido.fetch(..)``` and ```future.result(...)```.

issue_title: Add info for extending IndexView in the docs
issue_body:
Since #22 is fixed I recommend adding a paragraph in the README on how you can use a class inheriting from ``IndexView`` instead of ``('smoketest/', include('smoketest.urls'))`` so that you could override its ``dispatch`` method for permission checking (i.e check for caller's IP, add some Basic HTTP auth etc). 

This is a common requirement I think.

issue_title: Cannot use other template engines since version 3.0
issue_body:
In version 3.0, a lot of code was refactored, including ``render_to_temporary_file()`` (see PR #95). In that function, ``context`` is now converted into a ``Context`` object, which is a class related to Django's template language.

Django 1.8 introduced pluggable template engines and I had no problems using Jinja2 with version 2.0.3 of django-wkhtmltopdf.

This new code in ``render_to_temporary_file()`` causes problems with other template engines:

```python
# make sure the context is a context object
if not isinstance(context, (Context, RequestContext)):
    context = Context(context)

content = smart_text(template.render(context))
```

The ``render()`` method, according to [DEP 182](https://github.com/django/deps/blob/master/final/0182-multiple-template-engines.rst#backends-api), is actually supposed to take a ``dict``. The DTL backend's ``Template`` class handles converting the ``dict`` to a ``Context`` or ``RequestContext``.

Can this template engine dependency be removed?

I noticed issue #100 discusses warnings that are the result of the some of the changes above. I raised a separate issue since 3.0 is no longer usable for users of other template engines.

Thanks for your help.

issue_title: French translation for ForeignKeyAutocompleteAdmin broken
issue_body:
Hi,
It seems that the French translation for the help message appearing under the inputs with `ForeignKeyAutocompleteAdmin` does not work properly.

It says : `Use the left field to do xxx lookups in the fields xxx et xxx.` instead of the French translation.

I was able to fix it by editting the .po and regenerating the .mo file.
Btw, it also fixes a typo  (`recheres` --> `recherches`).

Here is diff for the .po which corrects the changes:

```
diff broken/django.po working/django.po
29,32c29,30
< msgid ""
< "Use the left field to do %(model_name)s lookups in the fields "
< "%(field_list)s."
< msgstr "Utilisez le champ de gauche pour faire des recheres de %(model_name)s dans les champs %(field_list)s."

---
> msgid "Use the left field to do %(model_name)s lookups in the fields %(field_list)s."
> msgstr "Utilisez le champ de gauche pour faire des recherches de %(model_name)s dans les champs %(field_list)s."
```

Thanks

issue_title: add do-not-watch configuration options
issue_body:
There are some cases where it is desirable to never watch a specific directory (https://github.com/facebook/buck/issues/616)

I haven't thought a huge amount about this, but there are two ways that come to mind:

* add a `.watchmanconfig` option that prevents watching a project tree (simplest for unprivileged users)
* add `/etc/watchman.json` option that prevents watches a list of paths (more suitable for administrators)

Behavior would be similar to https://facebook.github.io/watchman/docs/config.html#illegal_fstypes

issue_title: "json" 204 response causes migrate warning
Support only the 3.x release of jQuery Core
issue_body:
As of jQuery Migrate 3.0, this plugin should be paired only with Core 3.x and not older versions. That simplifies the work the plugin needs to do since it can depend on the behavior and APIs of the latest core and not have to work around all the quirks of previous versions. 

* [ ] Document this new usage in the README, which is to include the latest Core and Migrate in the old project. (Previously, we would tell users to include Migrate with the current version of Core they were using on the page.)
* [ ] Add a version check to ensure people don't misuse 3.0 by running it with older versions of jQuery.
* [ ] Update Migrate code to remove any Core-version-dependent code paths, for example the [checks whether internal functions are privatized](https://github.com/jquery/jquery-migrate/blob/master/src/css.js#L4)
* [ ] Determine and document the Core versions that we support for doing an upgrade. Presently it's 1.6.4 but this approach may make it possible to go back further?

issue_title: RelationField's don't seem to be persisted properly
issue_body:
I came across this creating a custom widget for selecting relations (for RelationChoice fields) but it seems to happen using the default widget too. The symptom is the same as this SO post:
http://stackoverflow.com/questions/27278300/relationlist-wrong-field-value-in-tile-schema

The objects stored in the tiles data are not RelationValues, but content objects, and become broken after a server restart.

That post gave some good hints, as far as I can tell the form handling for tiles is all done in plone.app.tiles rather than in z3c.form's applyForm(), so the correct IDataManager is never used.

Anyway, long story short, I've added this code to what seems like a logical place and it works for my widget and for the default one also. Branch is here:

https://github.com/hatchddigital/plone.app.tiles/commits/relationfield-handling

If this seems like a reasonable change let me know and I'll create a pull request, or if anyone has any hints or pointers as to a better fix I would appreciate that :).

issue_title: Margins removed in baseline
issue_body:
When I run or save a modified README code in Jupyter, I get a different image than the generated baseline.

Example code run in a Jupyter cell:
```python
#%%file test.py
%matplotlib inline

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pytest

@pytest.mark.mpl_image_compare
def test_succeeds():
    fig = plt.figure()
    ax = fig.add_subplot(1,1,1)
    ax.plot([1,2,3])
    fig.savefig("test")
    return fig
```

Running `test_succeeds();` in Jupyter gives resulting image and saved `test.png` **with margins**:

![test](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/10778668/25150075/b1f6e174-244e-11e7-9dc4-f1d4c126eaff.png)

Next, I generate a `test.py` file by uncommenting the first line, commenting the second line 
and then running the cell 

```python
%%file test.py
#%matplotlib inline

...
```
From a command prompt, in the appropriate directory I run the following to generate the baseline image:

    > py.test --mpl-generate-path=baseline test.py

In the baseline folder, a comparable image called `test_succeeds.png` is created **without margins**:

![test_succeeds](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/10778668/25150260/58d3509a-244f-11e7-9eeb-eaeca0c5648f.png)

Aside from the difference in sizes, I notice the baseline image uses the classic mpl style.  Perhaps this is the cause for the absence in margins.  I report this as an issue as it is ideal that when testing the baseline image, it should be the same image as shown and saved by Jupyter.


![2017-04-18 16_25_19-scratchpad 5](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/10778668/25151471/b9c6f5e2-2453-11e7-8440-488148dad383.png)

issue_title: Runtime change in parameters is not fully respected by luigi
issue_body:
Changing luigi parameters after a Task was instantiated apply to some extent (i.e. the pipeline works with single worker and static deps), but is not respected when one has dynamic dependencies (i.e. only the values passed to the constructor get sent to the worker) and also the string representation of a task shows only default values (or those passed to the constructor). Here's an example:
```python
>>> class MyTask(Task):
...     p = Parameter(1)

>>> t = MyTask(p=3)
>>> repr(t)
"MyTask(p=3)"

>>> t.p = 7
>>> repr(t)
"MyTask(p=3)"
>>> t.p
7
```
I suspect that this is kind of a design decision, but it's not very clearly stated in the docs, so maybe it is not. Anyway, was this behaviour intended? Do you think trying to fix it is a bad idea?

```
                                "aud": self.url,
                                "jti": self.random_string(64),
                                "exp": exp }
        assertion = jwt.encode(self.header, claim, self.key).decode('utf-8')
        headers = {'Content-Type': 'application/x-www-form-urlencoded'}
        payload = 'grant_type=urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:jwt-bearer&\
client_id={}&client_secret={}&assertion={}'\
                   .format(self.client_id, self.client_secret, assertion)
        response = requests.post(self.url, headers=headers, data=payload)
        self.access = json.loads(response.content)
        self.valid = int(time.time()) + self.access['expires_in']
        self.token = self.access['access_token']

    def random_string(self,length):
        return ''.join([random.choice(string.ascii_letters + string.digits) 
                    for n in range(length)])
```

issue_title: Change the _get_intra_event_std function of ASK14 (GMPE)
issue_body:
In the Abrahamson et al (2014) GMPE the `_get_intra_event_std` function raises an error if the `phi_al` term is less than the `phi_amp` term to avoid taking the square root of a negative number (https://github.com/gem/oq-engine/blob/master/openquake/hazardlib/gsim/abrahamson_2014.py#L364). This situation happens for IMTs with periods longer than 5 and for magnitudes smaller than about 4.3 - 4.4. 

The authors of the GMPE have addressed this in their hazard code, and simply set `phi_amp` to be `0.99 * phi_al` in this circumstance (https://github.com/abrahamson/HAZ/blob/master/HAZ%20files/NGAWest2.F#L3098). As raising an error causes the calculation to fail for reasons that might be cryptic to the users, I recommend to adopt the same adjustment here rather than raising an error.

issue_title: Potential documentation issue
issue_body:
Hello,

Firstly, I would like to say sorry if this is the incorrect way to submit a potential documentation problem. I went through your [readthedoc page](http://boto3.readthedocs.io/) but couldn't find any contact information.

In your document for [IAM.list_users method](http://boto3.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/services/iam.html#IAM.Client.list_users), you stated:

> `PasswordLastUsed` (datetime) --

> The date and time, in ISO 8601 date-time format , when the user's password was last used to sign in to an AWS website. For a list of AWS websites that capture a user's last sign-in time, see the Credential Reports topic in the Using IAM guide. If a password is used more than once in a five-minute span, only the first use is returned in this field. This field is null (not present) when:
> * The user does not have a password
> * The password exists but has never been used (at least not since IAM started tracking this information on October 20th, 2014 there is no sign-in data associated with the user

> This value is returned only in the GetUser and ListUsers actions.

IMHO, this means `user does not have a password` -> `no PasswordLastUsed` and thus it implies: `has PasswordLastUsed` -> `user has a password`.

Unfortunately, this overlooked an edge case.

When user had a password and used it to log in to AWS, the data of PasswordLastUsed will be set. However, if later on we delete password for this user, `list_users` and `get_user` still displays the info of `PasswordLastUsed`.

Thus, I think the information might be misleading.

During development of one of our tools, we actually based our work on the assumption that if a user has `PasswordLastUsed` field, he/she should have a password and it was wrong due to the edge case described above.

I think we can improve this by clarify about such case, and advise developers on using `get_login_profile` to confirm whether a user has an IAM password or not (which is what we settled to use in our project).

Best regards

issue_title: security group rule definition unclear
issue_body:
I'm trying to find documentation on the exact definition of a Security Group Rule. The AWS Service limits for an EC2-Classic SG say that the limit is 100 rules in the VPC User Guide:
https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonVPC/latest/UserGuide/VPC_SecurityGroups.html#VPC_Security_Group_Differences

I have a rule that has '115 Permission Entries' under the **InBound Rules Count** column in the console. 

A majority of the rules as seen in the console are single public ip addresses to ports 80, 443, and 22. 
```
type protocol port-range        source
ssh    tcp           22          x.x.x.x/32
http   tcp           80          x.x.x.x/32
https  tcp          443          x.x.x.x/32
ssh    tcp           22          y.y.y.y/32
http   tcp           80          y.y.y.y/32
https  tcp          443          y.y.y.y/32
```
The Boto3 EC2 security group resource has an attribute `ip_permissions` which is  
> a list of one or more inbound rules associated with the security group.
 
http://boto3.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/services/ec2.html#EC2.SecurityGroup.ip_permissions

However, the number of inbound rules reports as being 3 rather than 6. 
```python
>>> import boto3
 
>>>ec2 = boto3.resource('ec2')
>>>security_group = ec2.SecurityGroup('sg-123456')
>>>len(security_group.ip_permissions)
3
```

the output of `security_group.ip_permissions` groups the rules by the `FromPort` key and aggregates the list of source ips in the `IpRanges` key

```python
>>>security_group.ip_permissions
[
  {
     ...
     u'FromPort': 22,
     u'IpRanges': [{u'CidrIp': 'x.x.x.x/32'}, {u'CidrIp': 'y.y.y.y/32'}]
     ...
  },
  {
     ...
     u'FromPort': 80,
     u'IpRanges': [{u'CidrIp': 'x.x.x.x/32'}, {u'CidrIp': 'y.y.y.y/32'}]
     ...
  },
  {
     ...
     u'FromPort': 443,
     u'IpRanges': [{u'CidrIp': 'x.x.x.x/32'}, {u'CidrIp': 'y.y.y.y/32'}]
     ...
  }
]
```


I'm assuming that the service limit must be evaluating the rules the same way the boto3 client reports and not the console since the security group still allows adding additional "rules" in the console, but I haven't been able to find any documentation that explicitly says that.

This seems to be contradictory to the security group definition found in the [Amazon EC2 Security Groups for Linux Instances](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/using-network-security.html#security-group-rules) doc:

> For each rule, you specify the following:
>* Protocol: The protocol to allow. The most common protocols are 6 (TCP) 17 (UDP), and 1 (ICMP).
>* Port range : For TCP, UDP, or a custom protocol, the range of ports to allow. You can specify a single 
>* port number (for example, 22), or range of port numbers (for example, 7000-8000).
>* ICMP type and code: For ICMP, the ICMP type and code.
>* Source or destination: The source (inbound rules) or destination (outbound rules) for the traffi

Are distinct rules only calculated based on the Protocol, Port Range, and ICMP Type? 


It seems that the boto3 client and the AWS console report different values.

issue_title: Using camera_move action in feature layer is inaccurate
issue_body:
__Summary:__
The `camera_move` action introduces inaccuracy between the center of the `camera` feature in the `minimap` and the point that was passed as an argument to `camera_move`. The bigger the map, the more inaccurate it gets.

[This jupyter notebook](https://gist.github.com/islamelnabarawy/3466b6aadb5002782e0de3ac6a90ae29) illustrates the issue.

The inaccuracy is probably coming from the way the `camera` feature layer itself is generated, but it manifests very clearly when the center of the `camera` rectangle is used as an argument to `camera_move`.

__Steps to reproduce:__
- Initialize the API and connect to the feature layer, then start a new game using any map
- Use the `camera` feature from the `minimap` to calculate the center of the `camera` rectangle on the minimap
- Use the `camera_move` action to move the camera to that calculated point

__Expected result:__
- Camera shouldn't move and center of `camera` feature should remain unchanged.

__Actual result:__
- Camera drifts slightly towards the top-left corner
- Repeating the last two steps causes the camera to continue drifting towards the top-left corner

I am maintaining a project that still supports Python 2.6, and I was glad to see that commit 9989f89 added a workaround for `OrderedDict` in support of Python 2.6.

However, the [PyPI package information of GitPython 2.0.3](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/GitPython/2.0.3) does not mention Python 2.6.

issue_title: Incorrect arguments supplied to json.JSONEncoder.default
issue_body:
This happens when I try to trigger an incident with a non JSON serializable object in the 'details' dictionary:

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/home/mgrandi/rabbitmq_monitor/app/rabbitmq_monitor/pagerduty.py", line 56, in _trigger_incident
    client=self.client_name)
  File "/home/mgrandi/.pyenv/versions/rabbitmq_monitor_venv/lib/python3.5/site-packages/pygerduty/__init__.py", line 626, in trigger_incident
    client=client, client_url=client_url, contexts=contexts)
  File "/home/mgrandi/.pyenv/versions/rabbitmq_monitor_venv/lib/python3.5/site-packages/pygerduty/__init__.py", line 588, in create_event
    data=_json_dumper(data).encode('utf-8'),
  File "/home/mgrandi/.pyenv/versions/3.5.2/lib/python3.5/json/__init__.py", line 237, in dumps
    **kw).encode(obj)
  File "/home/mgrandi/.pyenv/versions/3.5.2/lib/python3.5/json/encoder.py", line 198, in encode
    chunks = self.iterencode(o, _one_shot=True)
  File "/home/mgrandi/.pyenv/versions/3.5.2/lib/python3.5/json/encoder.py", line 256, in iterencode
    return _iterencode(o, 0)
  File "/home/mgrandi/.pyenv/versions/rabbitmq_monitor_venv/lib/python3.5/site-packages/pygerduty/__init__.py", line 747, in _datetime_encoder
    return json.JSONEncoder.default(obj)
TypeError: default() missing 1 required positional argument: 'o'
```

While I realize that the error is because i'm doing something wrong, there seems to be a bug where the json.JSONEncoder.default(obj) call is not using the right arguments. The exception should be (for example)

```
>>> import uuid, json
>>> json.dumps({"lol": uuid.uuid4()})
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
  File "/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.5/lib/python3.5/json/__init__.py", line 230, in dumps
    return _default_encoder.encode(obj)
  File "/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.5/lib/python3.5/json/encoder.py", line 198, in encode
    chunks = self.iterencode(o, _one_shot=True)
  File "/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.5/lib/python3.5/json/encoder.py", line 256, in iterencode
    return _iterencode(o, 0)
  File "/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.5/lib/python3.5/json/encoder.py", line 179, in default
    raise TypeError(repr(o) + " is not JSON serializable")
TypeError: UUID('9c5c7b30-def2-4497-ba84-f49b409b072c') is not JSON serializable
>>>
```

It seems the bug is that since you are calling json.JSONEncoder.default, but you you aren't directly subclassing JSONEncoder, you need to pass 'self' in as well as 'o'

https://docs.python.org/3.5/library/json.html#json.JSONEncoder.default

issue_title: Public dependencies are not resolved properly on private packages

issue_title: pipenv lock --requirements --dev fails if -e . is installed
issue_body:

If you have a lock file with some dependency *and* did `pipenv install --dev -e .`, a `pipenv lock --requirements --dev` will fail like this:

```

    sys.exit(cli())

    return _process_result(sub_ctx.command.invoke(sub_ctx))
  File "/Users/hynek/.local/venvs/pipenv/lib/python3.6/site-packages/pipenv/vendor/click/core.py", line 895, in invoke
    return ctx.invoke(self.callback, **ctx.params)
  File "/Users/hynek/.local/venvs/pipenv/lib/python3.6/site-packages/pipenv/vendor/click/core.py", line 535, in invoke

  File "/Users/hynek/.local/venvs/pipenv/lib/python3.6/site-packages/pipenv/cli.py", line 245, in lock
    core.do_init(dev=dev, requirements=requirements)
  File "/Users/hynek/.local/venvs/pipenv/lib/python3.6/site-packages/pipenv/core.py", line 1367, in do_init
    skip_lock=skip_lock, verbose=verbose, concurrent=concurrent)

    click.echo('\n'.join(d[0] for d in sorted(dev_deps_list)))
TypeError: '<' not supported between instances of 'list' and 'tuple'
```

##### Describe your environment

1. OS Type macOS
1. Python version: `$ python -V` 3.6.4
1. Pipenv version: `$ pipenv --version` 11.0.1

##### Expected result

A list of dependencies.

##### Actual result

The traceback above. Adding `--verbose` doesn’t change the output.

##### Steps to replicate

1. Create an empty directory
2. Add a setup.py into it:

```
from setuptools import setup

setup(name="t", version="1.0", py_modules=["t"])
```
3. And an empty t.py for good measure: `touch t.py`
4. Install some stuff: `pipenv install --dev pytest`
5. Install current package: `pipenv install --dev -e .` (installing both at once leads to an error, pretty sure that’s a bug too?)
6. Finally call: `pipenv lock --verbose --requirements --dev` and get the traceback from above.

I tried to pdb it and the problem is that all deps in `dev_deps_list` are tuples except for `['# -e .', True, True]`.

This shouldn’t happen on Python 2 albeit it returns a wrong results (just try `(1,) < [2,]` in a Python 2 repl).

issue_title: 38.2.0 Breaks installs of several packages on 2.7 with UnicodeEncodeError parsing package METADATA
issue_body:
Using `setuptools==38.2.0` on Python 2.7.14, this `setup.py` will fail on **any** of the packages under `install_requires` with errors such as `UnicodeEncodeError: 'ascii' codec can't encode character u'\u2013' in position 4258: ordinal not in range(128)`.  None of these happened under `38.1.0` for instance.  Error is happening on this line: https://github.com/pypa/setuptools/blob/97ff22f31ace57f4eabb6f1e77c9c553de0d1c24/setuptools/wheel.py#L59 

```python
from setuptools import setup, find_packages

setup(
    name="test-setuptools-encoding-problem",
    version="1.0.0",
    packages=find_packages(),
    install_requires=[
        "requests==2.18.4",
        "eventbrite==3.3.3",
        "django_mathfilters==0.4.0",
        "django-countries==5.0",
    ]
)
```

issue_title: Better period extracting from dateutil parser
issue_body:
As we now have information of what date units (year, month, hour) [were parsed exactly](https://github.com/scrapinghub/dateparser/blob/c0b7340bb7e4f670c876d5d3e8863ffe5afcf8db/dateparser/date_parser.py#L51) by dateutil parser, we now can guess date period with more precision. For example, if any of the `day`, `hour`, `minute`, `second`, `microsecond` units were parsed, then it is `day` period, else if `month` was parsed then period is `month` and at last `year` period for `year` unit. I am not sure if `week` period can be applicable to dates passed to dateutil parser.

This period should be passed all the way back to call from `_DateLanguageParser`.

issue_title: Problem with compiling for Python 3.5 under Windows 10 x64
issue_body:
I had a problem when tried to compile this library. The problem was the following:
Compiler said thet the following definitions from **python-crfsuite/crfsuite/include/os.h**  conflict with such definitions from system <math.h>:
`#define isfinite    _finite`
`#define snprintf    _snprintf`

I changed them to the following:
`#define my_isfinite    _finite`
`#define my_snprintf    _snprintf`
And also fixed all places in the library where these definitions are used, and then everything compiled perfectly.

issue_title: Efficiently pulling latest message from a topic
issue_body:
I am interested in a method to efficiently pull the last message from an existing consumer

After a little bit of digging I've come up with the following incantation

```python
consumer = client = KafkaClient(hosts="xxxxxxx")
topic = client.topics['mytopic']
consumer = topic.get_simple_consumer()

def consume_latest():
     latest_offsets = [
           (partition, pykafka.common.OffsetType.LATEST) for partition in consumer._partitions.keys()
        ]
     consumer.reset_offsets(latest_offsets)
     # now step the offset back by one to fetch the latest...
     almost_latest_offsets = []
     for partition, owned_partition in consumer._partition.items():
         almost_latest_offsets.append(
              (partition, owned_partition.next_offset - 1)
          )

     consumer.reset_offsets(almost_latest_offsets)
     return consumer.consume()
```

which looks a little funky but to me but I _think_ it should work. However because of the funkiness I wanted to check if there might be better way to do it?

issue_title: Travis builds are slow
issue_body:
I noticed this during the EuSciPy sprint last night, but I'm new here so I'd love to hear the opinion of people who've actually worked on this.

So, for illustration, here's a recent Travis run: https://travis-ci.org/scikit-image/scikit-image/builds/77734058
If we look at the Python 2.7 build for that run, we see that it took about 20 minutes. Of that 20 minutes, nearly 7 minutes was consumed by the install step in `.travis.yml`:
```
install:
    - python setup.py build_ext --inplace
    - python setup.py install
```
If you look at the output for each of those commands, the compilation of extension modules appears to be happening in both. This was most recently changed in 095160f1c63327d25cd0f5c98c56f79b869f66cf. @blink1073: Can you comment on why this was needed? I think it might be possible to just run the `install` command.

I've started a `travis-speedups` branch in my fork to experiment with possible solutions.

issue_title: anomaly_likelihood_test: Has Broken Test (Fix is in comment)
issue_body:
## The text below contains both the description of the problem, and the fix to make the test work, and the code improvement of the class being tested.
 
**.../nupic/tests/unit/nupic/algorithms/anomaly_likelihood_test.py line#226**
testEstimateAnomalyLikelihoodsMalformedRecords() - ***actually tests nothing.***

#### the lines:

data1 = _generateSampleData(mean=0.2)
data1 = data1 + [(2, 2), (2, 2, 2, 2), (), (2)]  # Malformed records
    
likelihoods, avgRecordList, estimatorParams = (
      an.estimateAnomalyLikelihoods(data1[0:1000])
)

#### should be changed to:

data1 = _generateSampleData(mean=0.2)
data1 = data1**[0:996]** + [(2, 2), (2, 2, 2, 2), (), (2)]  # Malformed records
    
likelihoods, avgRecordList, estimatorParams = (
      an.estimateAnomalyLikelihoods(data1[0:1000])
)

... so that when estimateAnomalyLikelihoods() is called with **data1[0:1000]** , the "malformed" records will be included.

##### A corresponding change is needed in anomaly_likelihood.py in order to have the 2nd to last record not cause an exception (i.e. the "()" in  [(2, 2), (2, 2, 2, 2), (), (2)]). 

_This is so because the test method as previously written never actually passed the above malformed records in to the method being tested; now that they are being passed in, the code which "detects" bad records is now being executed._

**The required change is on line#486 in the _anomalyScoreMovingAverage() method:**

#### Instead of:
if len(record) != 3:
   if verbosity >= 0:
      print "Malformed record:", record
      continue

#### Should be:
if **not isinstance(record, list) or** len(record) != 3:
   if verbosity >= 0:
      print "Malformed record:", record
      continue

issue_title: [Discussion] Radio button bar functionality - is it "vanilla"?
issue_body: I've put together a Django Widget which allows radio buttons to be displayed as a [Bootstrap 4 button group](https://getbootstrap.com/docs/4.1/components/button-group/). My team has found this quite useful for mobile-friendly sites. I know Bootstrap 4 tries to stay with "vanilla bootstrap", but I'm not sure if this would qualify and figured I'd bring it up for discussion with the team. ```python class RadioSelectButtonGroup(RadioSelect): """ This widget renders a Bootstrap 4 set of buttons horizontally instead of typical radio buttons. Much more mobile friendly. """ template_name = 'bootstrap4/widgets/radio_select_button_group.html' ``` This could use some improvement (such as taking "primary" as an attribute), but you'll get the general idea: ```django {% for group, options, index in widget.optgroups %} {% for option in options %} {% endfor %} {% endfor %} ``` To call it in a form: ```python from bootstrap4.widgets import RadioSelectButtonGroup class MyForm(forms.Form): BUY = 1 SELL = 2 BUY_TO_COVER = 3 SELL_SHORT = 4 TRANSACTIONS = ( (BUY, 'Buy'), (SELL, 'Sell'), (BUY_TO_COVER, 'Buy to Cover'), (SELL_SHORT, 'Sell Short'), ) order_type = forms.ChoiceField( help_text="Select the order type.", required=True, label="Order Type:", widget=RadioSelectButtonGroup, choices=TRANSACTIONS, initial=BUY, ) ``` Here's what it is rendered as: ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/68164/43079700-ca2e0fe2-8e5b-11e8-8c9a-f098aa451d6d.png) Would this be worth including? If so, I'd be happy to whip up a P.R.

Hi,

I was running tests on my code today involving Mailjet API calls and was surprised to get this warning:
```
Unverified HTTPS request is being made. Adding certificate verification is strongly advised. See: https://urllib3.readthedocs.io/en/latest/security.html
```

Regards !

issue_title: Invenio to become HTML5 ready
issue_body:
Originally

By following e.g. the book *HTML5 Up and Running* (by Mark Pilgrim, O'Reilly) (which in the end is the same of [Diving into HTML5](http://diveintohtml5.org/)), one can easily employ new HTML5 tags and conventions to Invenio output to exploit new semantical features.

This would consist of:
* [x] adding `<!DOCTYPE html>` at the beginning
* [x] having `<html lang="en">` (with appropriate lang parameter)
* [x] simply having `<meta charset="utf-8">` in the head tag
* [ ] nicely exploiting `rel=` arguments to link in the header. (this is indeed a complex task, as it would be nice to properly map the breadcrumb structure (with the `rel="up"` link), and the search results paging (with the `rel="first"`, `rel="next"`, `rel="prev"` and `rel="last"`
* [ ] employing `<article>` to tag a record in search results (or detailed record)
* [ ] employing `<nav>` to tag the menu bar
* [ ] employing `<footer>` to tag the typical Invenio footer
* [ ] using `<time>` in every record displayed to semantically tag the *publication date* from MARC, (or *record creation date* when publication date is missing)
* [ ] In IE<9 HTML5 tags are not recognized and can't be rendered but with the [HTML5 enabling script](http://code.google.com/p/html5shim/) must be included.
* [ ] adding the `placeholder` attribute in most input boxes to give hints on how to fill them
* [ ] adding `autofocus` flag, when needed (e.g. on the search box)
* [ ] adding `type="search"` attribute to the Invenio search box
* [ ] adding *microdata*. Among those, *[reviews](http://diveintohtml5.org/extensibility.html#review)* microdata can directly fall into the core of Invenio. (people, events, organizations really depends on the type of records)

More improvements, of course, can be added :-)

issue_title: rest: invalid deposit class detection when missing pid_value view argument
issue_body:
On POST calls, since `request.view_args['pid_value']` is not part of the url (`/api/deposit/depositions`), the [detected deposit class](https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-deposit/blob/master/invenio_deposit/links.py#L74-L76) always falls back to `invenio_deposit.api.Deposit`, causing any additional action links added using the `invenio_deposit.urils.mark_as_action` decorator to be skipped.

Perhaps adding a `pid_type -> deposit_class` mapping as part of a `current_records_rest.default_pid_classes` might help to determine the desired default:

```python
deposit_cls = current_records_rest.default_pid_classes.get(pid.pid_type, Deposit)
if 'pid_value' in request.view_args:
    deposit_cls = request.view_args['pid_value'].data[1].__class__
```

issue_body:
Reading the docs seems this library is made to work to ZODB, however, I'm trying to use this simply as an in memory BTree to store some ordered data and be able to efficiently query it after, since it's implemented in C (or the critical parts at least) it seems to yield slightly better results than using a simple python list with a bisect algorithm, not to mention a much cleaner syntax for range searches.

Am I using this wrong since it's optimized around ZODB? Should I consider some other implementation?

issue_title: Enhance error message for not activated user
Activation token invalidated by user login
issue_body:
Hello, thanks for the great work on this library!

`ActivationView` uses Django's `PasswordResetTokenGenerator` to generate the activation token. To create this token, this class hashes internal user state, including the user's last login time. This means if the user logs in before clicking the activation link (in situations where the activation link is sent, but the user is not required to click it before logging in), the token is invalidated and will not work. The API returns a 400 with  `INVALID_TOKEN_ERROR`. 

This token hashing behavior makes sense for password reset tokens, however when creating activation tokens we should not hash the user's last login. This will allow djoser to support optional activation/confirmation links.

What are your thoughts on this? Would you be willing to accept a pull request?

issue_title: Race condition in case of near simultaneous POST API calls
issue_body:
Some of Djoser views are vulnerable to a race condition (which is not really a security issue unless you've setup debug in production). Basically saying, the problem is that in case of near simultaneous requests to endpoint with a POST method, which creates object for a model with a field with unique constraint, it will result in an `IntegrityError` and will return 500 to the client. 

It happens because of mentioned race condition, where validation step passes for both requests and than it fails on transaction to database.

issue_title: Travis download caching
issue_body:
It would be great if we could cache some of the heavy download (Android SDK and NDK and CrystaX NDK) in Travis. That should not only speed up build but also make them more reliable.

We could either try to bind a Docker image directory with the Travis file system and cache it. Or directly cache the entire Docker build. I've played with caching a Docker container, but this is not built-in in Travis and needs to be done in a kind of hacky way which is painful.
So we may take a look at the other solution i.e. mounting a download directory and try to cache it first.

issue_title: World Bank remote data error: need new url?
issue_body:
I am running the Anaconda distribution of Python3. I made sure I sure that pandas_datareader was up to date by running:

`pip install -U pandas_datareader`

Then I tried running the following:

```
from pandas_datareader import wb
wb.download(indicator='SP.POP.TOTL', country='WLD', start=1800, end=2100)
```

Upon which I get the error, traceback:

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/Users/jche11/anaconda/lib/python3.6/site-packages/IPython/core/interactiveshell.py", line 2862, in run_code
    exec(code_obj, self.user_global_ns, self.user_ns)
  File "<ipython-input-2-1a0b0bf45d07>", line 2, in <module>
    wb.download(indicator='SP.POP.TOTL', country='WLD', start=1800, end=2100)
  File "/Users/jche11/anaconda/lib/python3.6/site-packages/pandas_datareader/wb.py", line 379, in download
    **kwargs).read()
  File "/Users/jche11/anaconda/lib/python3.6/site-packages/pandas_datareader/wb.py", line 163, in read
    return self._read()
  File "/Users/jche11/anaconda/lib/python3.6/site-packages/pandas_datareader/wb.py", line 172, in _read
    df = self._read_one_data(self.url + indicator, self.params)
  File "/Users/jche11/anaconda/lib/python3.6/site-packages/pandas_datareader/base.py", line 81, in _read_one_data
    out = self._get_response(url, params=params).json()
  File "/Users/jche11/anaconda/lib/python3.6/site-packages/pandas_datareader/base.py", line 139, in _get_response
    raise RemoteDataError('Unable to read URL: {0}'.format(url))
pandas_datareader._utils.RemoteDataError: Unable to read URL: http://api.worldbank.org/countries/WLD/indicators/SP.POP.TOTL?date=1800%3A2100&per_page=25000&format=json
```

Copying that last [url](http://api.worldbank.org/countries/WLD/indicators/SP.POP.TOTL?date=1800%3A2100&per_page=25000&format=json) into a browser results in a request error from that page.

I looked at the World Bank API [documentation](https://datahelpdesk.worldbank.org/knowledgebase/articles/898581-api-basic-call-structure), and it appears that the base url is now http://api.worldbank.org/v2/

I modified the url from the error message to add "/v2"  after worldbank.org:

http://api.worldbank.org/v2/countries/WLD/indicators/SP.POP.TOTL?date=1800%3A2100&per_page=25000&format=json

Copying that into a browser successfully returned data

```
from pandas_datareader import data 
data.Options('aapl','yahoo')
```

```
import pandas_datareader as pdr
pdr.Options('aapl','yahoo')
```

issue_title: Parsing latitude/longitude for point
issue_body:
'''52 4 0 77 N 0 37 58 62 W'''
Is currently being misread when a point is created.
It appears to be taking the factions of a second in the latitude and reading it as the degrees of longitude.

It also does work if a decimal point is supplied though the result is in a different location to the previous error.
'''52 4 0.77 N 0 37 58.62 W'''

'''52 4' 0.77'' N 0 37' 58.62'' W''' does work but the previous two formats are unfortunately very common.

Perhaps this is a reasonable new feature?

I have also seen 52010077N 00375862W or similar supplied.

issue_title: New release for pyfolio
issue_body:
It looks like [enough has changed](https://github.com/quantopian/pyfolio/compare/v0.7.0...master) on pyfolio to merit v0.8.0 (with risk and performance attribution, I think we might even want to call it v1.0.0!)

We are also seeing an increasing number of users running into bugs that have already been patched: upping the version would make it easier to diagnose errors (both ours, and open source users).

Major highlights:
- Risk tear sheet #391 
- Simple tear sheet #389 
- Rolling annual volatility plot #390 
- Yahoo + pandas data reader bug fix #395 
- `information_ratio` [compatibility with empyrical](https://github.com/quantopian/pyfolio/commit/a34222e6a065b85147e9e7e53cce4549058bd51e) 

To-do:
- [ ] Performance attribution tear sheet #400 (still WIP, will definitely want to wait for this)
- [ ] Example notebooks for documentation #411 (ready for review and merge)
- [ ] Fama-French multivariate regression bug fix #406 (fairly serious bug fix, but Travis is sad)
- [ ] Resolve any dependency issues with zipline/alphalens/empyrical
- [ ] Do we want to outsource Fama-French functionality from pyfolio to empyrical? [#56 on empyrical](https://github.com/quantopian/empyrical/pull/56)
- [ ] Do we want to include returns decomposition from alphalens into pyfolio? [#183 on alphalens](https://github.com/quantopian/alphalens/pull/183)

@twiecki @gusgordon @richafrank @Jstauth thoughts? If this sounds good I'll organize a milestone :)

issue_title: Add a util funtion to construct factor_data with pre-computed forward returns dataframe
issue_body:
In `get_clean_factor_and_forward_returns`, the step for computing forward returns `merged_data = compute_forward_returns(prices, periods, filter_zscore)` takes a large proportion of time. In my opinion, it is rather common to change the factor column, while keeping the forward return columns unchanged, as we want to test different kinds of factors on the same set of forward returns. Currently, the user has to do this by passing a new factor series and rerunning `get_clean_factor_and_forward_returns`. This process can be painfully slow as the pricing data increases. Is it a good idea to add some functions to allow users to construct factor_data with a pre-computed forward returns dataframe and a factor series? I think this would be more flexible, while the current `get_clean_factor_and_forward_returns` does not have to change. Alternatively, is there any way to make `compute_forward_returns` faster? issue_title: Expect logging._srcfile to be None
issue_body:
The `breadcrumbs` module uses the `logging._srcfile` attribute and crashes if it is `None`. The `logging` module sort of allows this, though:

> \# _srcfile is only used in conjunction with sys._getframe().
> \# To provide compatibility with older versions of Python, set _srcfile
> \# to None if _getframe() is not available; this value will prevent
> \# findCaller() from being called.

We use a third party library that actively sets `logging._srcfile` to `None`. I'm aware that asking raven to consider this is rather weird, but I thought I'd at least ask :smile: 

I can provide a simple patch that makes `_wrap_logging_method` bail in that case, if there is interest.

issue_title: Add ability to draw from arbitrary distributions in RandomWalk profile
issue_body:
Currently we take the limit `nsep -> infinity` in order to draw from a Gaussian rather than actually doing a step-by-step random walk.  This is a very good approximation, even for low number of steps.

Consider extending this to allow drawing from other distributions, the most natural being other surface brightness profiles.

issue_title: Javascript Regex Literal Pattern Format Support (Perl Format)
issue_body:
### Description
For the pattern field on parameters, the validation performed seems to be expecting a string format of a regular expression (surrounded by quotes) instead of the format of a Javascript Regex Literal (surrounded by forward slashes).

### Expected bahaviour
I'm expecting connexion to evaluate a regex in the form of `/[a-zA-Z0-9]+/` ,the format of a Javascript Regex Literal in the [Json Schema Standard](http://json-schema.org/latest/json-schema-validation.html#anchor33)

### Actual behaviour
If I give the regex in a string form, `"[a-zA-Z0-9]+"`, everything behaves as normal. However, if I give the regex in the literal form `/[a-zA-Z0-9]+/`, then I receive a 400 with the following response.

```
{
  "status": 400,
  "type": "about:blank",
  "detail": "u'UBU6' does not match '/[a-zA-Z0-9]+/'\n\nFailed validating 'pattern' in schema:\n    {'description': 'Filter by instrument symbology. Full instrument symbology needs to be provided.',\n     'in': 'query',\n     'name': 'symbol',\n     'pattern': '/[a-zA-Z0-9]+/',\n     'type': 'string'}\n\nOn instance:\n    u'UBU6'",
  "title": "Bad Request"
}

```

### Steps to reproduce
Simply create a spec using the literal format of a pattern field.


### Additional info:

Output of the commands:

- `python --version` => Python 2.7.6
- `pip show connexion | grep "^Version\:"` => Version: 1.0.103

SQL API is adding new end points that allow data to stream to or from a table. This could be advantageous in multiple ways: no more batch uploading (breaking up table, setting schema, re-combining temp tables, etc.) and potentially not writing files but streaming the data directly using a multi-part HTTP handler.

issue_title: User don't have KEY field which breaks integrity
issue_body:
jira.group_members("jira-users") returns list of users where KEY field is actually NOT a user's key but a user's name!
But this field - user's name can be changed in JIRA by administrator and this causes big problem if we want to extract the data and keep the references between the entities.

Example:
1.you exported users to your database
2.you exported issues and linked them to the users (you should use issue.fields.assignee.name, as you cannot use fields.assignee.key because you don't know the user's keys)
3.you changed user's name
4.you exported new users (new user is created which is actually a duplicate)
5. you trying to sync issues and link them to users and new issues are linked to new user.
Now you have duplicate users both with issues!

Suggestion:
jira.group_members() should use user's key for keys and user's name field should be among other user's fields together with email and fullname.

JIRA's API already supports this - 
"/rest/api/2/group?groupname=#{groupname}&expand=users"  returns JSON with users' keys.

Please fix this.

issue_title: The instability problem of mouse operations in 'Fly Mode'
issue_body: In the `Fly Mode` the mouse_wheel operation still works but become very slow and sometimes it will give an `IndexError: list index out of range`. So maybe we could just remove the mouse_wheel operation and expect users to use just keyboard to control (actually there is no need for them to use mouse under this mode).

issue_title: Django 1.10 Middlewares compatibility
issue_body: The current [middlewares](django_toolkit/middlewares.py) are made for Django<=1.9. Django 1.0 has a [new interface for middlewares](https://docs.djangoproject.com/es/1.10/topics/http/middleware/), so we should start using the `django.utils.deprecation.MiddlewareMixin`.

issue_title: Handling lines in pdfminer Plane may require modifications
issue_body:
In #7, we significantly reduced the size of this repository by cutting `pdfminer` out of our repo and simply installing it as a dependency. Unfortunately, in the `extract_tables` utility, it appears that we needed modifications to the pdfminer library itself. Specifically, if we use `pdfminer.six` as is, we get this error:

```
[INFO] __main__ - Processing 1-s2.0-S003101829800159X-main.pdf (50 of 132)
ERROR:pdftotree.pdf.pdf_parsers:(184.194, 184.194, 50)
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/home/lwhsiao/repos/pdftotree/pdftotree/pdf/pdf_parsers.py", line 68, in cluster_vertically_aligned_boxes
    plane.extend(boxes)
  File "/home/lwhsiao/repos/pdftotree/.venv2/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pdfminer/utils.py", line 341, in extend
    self.add(obj)
  File "/home/lwhsiao/repos/pdftotree/.venv2/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pdfminer/utils.py", line 346, in add
    for k in self._getrange((obj.x0, obj.y0, obj.x1, obj.y1)):
  File "/home/lwhsiao/repos/pdftotree/.venv2/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pdfminer/utils.py", line 334, in _getrange
    for x in drange(x0, x1, self.gridsize):
  File "/home/lwhsiao/repos/pdftotree/.venv2/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pdfminer/utils.py", line 171, in drange
    assert v0 < v1, str((v0, v1, d))
AssertionError: (184.194, 184.194, 50)
```

when we try and extend the Plane with a box like 
```
<LTTextLineHorizontal 184.194,683.116,184.194,693.800 '(cid:211)\n'>]
```

This assertion is modified in the [original TreeStructure repo](https://github.com/HazyResearch/TreeStructure/blob/3bdd143358f3ec24d040c3aaa42fbbd2d10ab7aa/table-extraction/pdfminer/utils.py#L121-L125)

```py
def drange(v0, v1, d):
    """Returns a discrete range."""
    assert v0 <= v1
    return xrange(int(v0)//d, int(v1+d)//d)
```
to be `<=` rather than just `<`. There are likely other changes that were made to pdfminer itself in the TreeStructure repository. We should try and understand those changes, and see if it's possible to avoid them so that we don't have to have a duplicate of that library in our repo.

issue_title: Left multiplication of a matrix by a vector
issue_body:
DyNet currently supports matrix-vector multiplication, but does not support vector-matrix multiplication.

For example, we can multiply a 2x3 matrix and a length-3 vector to obtain a length-2 vector,
```
>>> import dynet as dy
>>> v = dy.inputVector([0, 0, 0])
>>> w = dy.inputMatrix([0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0], (2, 3))
>>> result = w * v
>>> result.npvalue()
array([ 0.,  0.])
```
but we cannot multiply a length-2 vector and a 2x3 matrix to obtain a length-3 vector,
```
>>> import dynet as dy
>>> v = dy.inputVector([0, 0])
>>> w = dy.inputMatrix([0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0], (2, 3))
>>> result = v * w
libc++abi.dylib: terminating with uncaught exception of type std::invalid_argument: Mismatched input dimensions in MatrixMultiply: [{2} {2,3}]
Abort trap: 6
```

Using `dynet.transpose`, we can write
```
>>> import dynet as dy
>>> v = dy.inputVector([0, 0])
>>> w = dy.inputMatrix([0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0], (2, 3))
>>> result = dy.transpose(w) * v
>>> result.npvalue()
array([ 0.,  0.,  0.])
```
but `dynet/expr.h` mentions that `transpose` is an O(n) operation, so this seems like it would be an inefficient workaround.

Alternatively, we can also write
```
>>> import dynet as dy
>>> v = dy.inputVector([0, 0])
>>> w = dy.inputMatrix([0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0], (2, 3))
>>> result = dy.transpose(v) * w
>>> result.npvalue()
array([[ 0.,  0.,  0.]])
```
but this returns a 1x3 matrix instead of a length-3 vector.

Would it be possible to add support for this? Or does the existing API already support vector-matrix multiplication by other means?

issue_title: Upgrade to pycountry 17.5.14
assertRaises cannot be used as a context manager
issue_body:
Python's unittest.TestCase.assertRaises can be used as a context manager as of Python 2.7. This is nice because it makes the code much easier to read when the call being made in even moderately complicated.

The workaround is to make a new callable that wraps the complicated call, but a context manager would be preferable.

If there is no objection to this feature then I'd be happy to implement it.

issue_title: Don't use "unittest2" for Python 3
issue_body:
testtools require unittest2 for testing, but unittest2 "is no longer needed" for Python 3 (quoted from https://pypi.python.org/pypi/unittest2). Please update requirements file to conditionally require unittest2 only for older Python version.

Each additional package makes distribution maintainers life more complicated, since they have to maintain yet another package even if the distribution only includes Python 3.5 or even 3.6.

Thanks.

issue_title: Don't use "linecache2" for recent Python 3 versions
issue_body:
traceback2 requires linecache2, but linecache2 is a backport (unfortunately it does not tell from which Python version, see https://github.com/testing-cabal/linecache2/issues/13). Please update the requirements file to conditionally require linecache2 only for older Python version.

Each additional package makes distribution maintainers life more complicated, since they have to maintain yet another package even if the distribution only includes Python 3.5 or even 3.6

Thanks.

issue_title: Documentation for pytest modules can currently not be generated  automatically with sphinx
issue_body:
See PR #126 

> It is worth noting that the autogenerated .rsts will probably need some more love in terms of formatting and streamlining. In addition, the actual pytest modules that make up the testing suite are not being automatically documented as of yet. This is primarily due to pytest conventions conflicting with sphinx implementation. In other words, sphinx cannot import pytest modules without init.py. This issue details the problems and discussions associated with that.

issue_title: [Documentation Issue] errors in DNS API example usage
issue_body:
Page Name: dns-usage
Release: 0.16.0

As noted at https://groups.google.com/d/msg/cloud-dns-discuss/m5CHw1ncS14/lTKvD7O_BgAJ

this example of the DNS API doesn't actually work:

```python
>>> import time
>>> from gcloud import dns
>>> client = dns.Client(project='PROJECT_ID')
>>> zone = client.zone('acme-co', 'example.com')
>>> TWO_HOURS = 2 * 60 * 60  # seconds
>>> record_set = zone.resource_record_set(
...    'www.example.com', 'CNAME', TWO_HOURS, 'www1.example.com')
>>> changes = zone.changes()
>>> changes.add_record_set(record_set)
>>> changes.create()  # API request
>>> while changes.status != 'done':
...     print('Waiting for changes to complete')
...     time.sleep(60)     # or whatever interval is appropriate
...     changes.reload()   # API request
```

Needs to be in this form:

CNAME:

```python
record_set = zone.resource_record_set('www.example.com.', 'CNAME',
                                      3600, ['www1.example.com.',])
```

Or for A records:

```python
record_set = zone.resource_record_set('www.example.com.', 'A',
                                      3600, ['11.111.11.111',])
```

Resource records need to be in a List form, DNS records seem to need the trailing dot.

issue_title: CloudLoggingHandler disregards contextual information
issue_labels: [{'id': 311535631, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/API:%20logging', 'name': 'API: logging', 'color': 'c0c0c0', 'default': False}, {'id': 544604235, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/Status:%20Acknowledged', 'name': 'Status: Acknowledged', 'color': 'fef2c0', 'default': False}, {'id': 77438584, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/Type:%20Question', 'name': 'Type: Question', 'color': '8bc34a', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
## Context

Python logging provides useful facilities to inject arbitrary contextual information into log records. I.e. the core logger's methods like `debug`, `info`, etc. can be provided with an optional parameter called `extra`. [Reference to the doc](https://docs.python.org/3/library/logging.html#logging.Logger.debug). There are also [more sophisticated techniques](https://docs.python.org/3/howto/logging-cookbook.html#adding-contextual-information-to-your-logging-output). It sounds extremely useful given stackdriver is quite friendly to structured data. 

## Problem

Unfortunately, `google.cloud.logging.handlers.CloudLoggingHandler` (or, strictly saying, underlying transports) disregards whatever is passed in `extra` parameter and sends further only `message`. Code reference [1](https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/blob/master/logging/google/cloud/logging/handlers/transports/background_thread.py#L139) and [2](https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/blob/master/logging/google/cloud/logging/handlers/transports/sync.py#L42). This is quite sad since `log_struct` does let us pass arbitrary structured entries.

## Proposed solution

Although a bit hacky, it would probably be the easiest to pass the whole `__dict__` of `LogRecord` object to `log_struct` [here](https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/blob/master/logging/google/cloud/logging/handlers/transports/background_thread.py#L139). I mean smth. like this:

```
self.batch.log_struct(record.__dict__, severity=record.levelname)
```

A property called `message` will automatically be there as well ([LogRecord attributes](https://docs.python.org/3/library/logging.html#logrecord-attributes)).

Ideally, such useful `log_struct`'s parameters as `labels`, `insert_id`, etc. should be recognized as well.

```
self.batch.log_struct(
    record.__dict__, 
    severity=record.levelname,
    labels=getattr(record, 'labels', None),
    insert_id=getattr(record, 'insert_id', None)
)
```

issue_title: spanner library doesn't work with credentials whose scope can't be modified.
issue_body:
I'm using application default credentials to create credentials for use with the spanner client library. These credentials are obtained by running 

```
gcloud auth application-default login
```

My code fails with the following stack trace.

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/bazel_cache/_bazel_jlewi/1c9ff9fd5966eae5ae66af2948992bce/bazel-sandbox/3357018790234970416/execroot/org_tensorflow_tensorboard/bazel-out/local-fastbuild/bin/tensorboard/platforms/gcp/spanner_e2e_test.runfiles/org_tensorflow_tensorboard/tensorboard/platforms/gcp/spanner_e2e_test.py", line 57, in testInsertSql
    (rowid, customer_number, run_id, event_log_id, path, offset))
  File "/bazel_cache/_bazel_jlewi/1c9ff9fd5966eae5ae66af2948992bce/bazel-sandbox/3357018790234970416/execroot/org_tensorflow_tensorboard/bazel-out/local-fastbuild/bin/tensorboard/platforms/gcp/spanner_e2e_test.runfiles/org_tensorflow_tensorboard/tensorboard/db.py", line 390, in execute
    self._delegate.execute(sql, parameters)
  File "/bazel_cache/_bazel_jlewi/1c9ff9fd5966eae5ae66af2948992bce/bazel-sandbox/3357018790234970416/execroot/org_tensorflow_tensorboard/bazel-out/local-fastbuild/bin/tensorboard/platforms/gcp/spanner_e2e_test.runfiles/org_tensorflow_tensorboard/tensorboard/platforms/gcp/spanner.py", line 221, in execute
    with self.database.batch() as batch:
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/google/cloud/spanner/database.py", line 401, in __enter__
    session = self._session = self._database._pool.get()
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/google/cloud/spanner/pool.py", line 228, in get
    session.create()
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/google/cloud/spanner/session.py", line 98, in create
    api = self._database.spanner_api
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/google/cloud/spanner/database.py", line 165, in spanner_api
    credentials = credentials.with_scopes((SPANNER_DATA_SCOPE,))
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/google/oauth2/credentials.py", line 119, in with_scopes
    'OAuth 2.0 Credentials can not modify their scopes.')
NotImplementedError: OAuth 2.0 Credentials can not modify their scopes.
```

The problem appears to be the following code from [database.py](https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/blob/master/spanner/google/cloud/spanner/database.py#L164)

```
    @property
    def spanner_api(self):
        """Helper for session-related API calls."""
        if self._spanner_api is None:
            credentials = self._instance._client.credentials
            if isinstance(credentials, google.auth.credentials.Scoped):
                credentials = credentials.with_scopes((SPANNER_DATA_SCOPE,))
            self._spanner_api = SpannerClient(
                lib_name='gccl',
                lib_version=__version__,
                credentials=credentials,
            )
        return self._spanner_api
```
My credential is of type [google.oauth2.credentials.Credentials](https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-auth-library-python/blob/master/google/oauth2/credentials.py#L118) which doesn't implement with_scopes.

The docs don't indicate a preferred pattern for distinguishing between credentials whose scope can be modified and those that can't. A simple solution would be to catch the NotImplementedError and ignore it.

issue_title: Update logging.list_entries to use the `resourceNames` field
issue_labels: [{'id': 311535631, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/api:%20logging', 'name': 'api: logging', 'color': 'd4af37', 'default': False}, {'id': 544604243, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/priority:%20p2+', 'name': 'priority: p2+', 'color': 'ffee58', 'default': False}, {'id': 544604235, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/status:%20acknowledged', 'name': 'status: acknowledged', 'color': 'fef2c0', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
It looks like `projectIds` was deprecated -- not sure when, but appears so (see https://cloud.google.com/logging/docs/api/reference/rest/v2/entries/list). It'll still work, so we're not in any major time crunch here, but we should swap out to using the `resourceNames` field when sending stuff to the API.

Ideally, this method would accept both `projects`, `organizations`, and generic `resources`. `projects` would just be prefixed with `projects/`, same for organizations, and resources just appended to the list of resource names where we assume that the person understands the format.

If we're going to swap the order of the parameters (ie, put `organizations` after `projects`) then this might be considered a breaking change :(

issue_title: Setting a timeout for BigQuery async_query doesn't work
issue_body:
The documentation and code appears to allow for setting a maximum duration before timing out for an async_query by passing `timeout` (an int for the number of milliseconds) to the `result()` function that is called on a query job (https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/blob/master/bigquery/google/cloud/bigquery/job.py#L476 and https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/blob/master/bigquery/google/cloud/bigquery/job.py#L1311). Similarly, there also appear to be some references to the `fetch_data()` function of a QueryResults object being able to accept a timeout parameter (https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/blob/master/bigquery/google/cloud/bigquery/query.py#L390).

However, passing a timeout value of 1, 0, or -1 in either fashion with a async_query does nothing to interrupt the query from being completed. Queries complete regardless and output results:

```
from google.cloud import bigquery
import uuid
client = bigquery.Client(project=project)
query_job = client.run_async_query(str(uuid.uuid4()),
                                   "select 'hello' as a, 32423432 as b")
query_job.begin()
query_results = query_job.result(timeout=1)
data = query_results.fetch_data(timeout_ms=1)
rows = list(data)
print(rows)
```
Output:
```
[(u'hello', 32423432)]
```

By contrast, setting a timeout value for a sync_query by setting the `timeout_ms` property of a QueryJob indeed works as expected as raises the appropriate JobComplete = False flag:

```
from google.cloud import bigquery
client = bigquery.Client(project=project)
query_job = client.run_sync_query("select 'hello' as a, 32423432 as b")
query_job.timeout_ms=1
query_job.run()
print(query_job._properties.get("jobComplete"))
rows = list(query_job.rows)
print(rows)
```
Output:
```
False
[]
```

It appears timeout for async_queries is still to be implemented and it is unclear how to access a JobIncomplete property via an async_query, forcing users to continue using sync_queries if they want the ability to time out their queries.

cc: @tswast who requested I raise the issue which I identified in this pandas-gbq PR: https://github.com/pydata/pandas-gbq/pull/25#discussion_r143263462

OSX
Python 2.7.13
google-cloud-python 0.27.0 and google-cloud-bigquery 0.26.0

issue_title: Reconsider tag name scheme for RTD
issue_body:
Using `umbrella-0.20.0` did not kick off a `stable` docs build on RTD. From their [documentation](http://docs.readthedocs.io/en/latest/versions.html), a `stable` build is only done when a semver-valid tag is pushed:

> You should push a tag for each version of your project. These tags should be numbered in a way that is consistent with [semantic versioning](http://semver.org/). This will map to your stable branch by default.

I tried to force it [to rebuild](https://readthedocs.org/projects/google-cloud-python/builds/4453937/) but it stuck with the last valid tag at 4d66524a57d73742b5fe1f4ca571b305031ea5d2.

issue_title: Subscriber can effectively deadlock itself when there are pending requests.
issue_labels: [{'id': 139685153, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/api:%20pubsub', 'name': 'api: pubsub', 'color': 'f8f8f8', 'default': False}, {'id': 544604244, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/priority:%20p1', 'name': 'priority: p1', 'color': 'ffa03e', 'default': False}, {'id': 486182624, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/release%20blocking', 'name': 'release blocking', 'color': 'ffa03e', 'default': False}, {'id': 77438580, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/type:%20bug', 'name': 'type: bug', 'color': 'd93f0b', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
Due to emergent behavior between three different systems:

1. Re-establishing the stream on error.
2. Pausing the stream when over load.
3. Factoring request queue size in load.

The subscriber can deadlock itself if it tries to re-establish a stream while the stream is paused due to request pressure, for example:

```
===== Subscriber Monitor =====
Messages processed: 1659
Rate: 0.82 Messages/second
Run time: 0:33:35.001386
Load: 1.67
p99 ack: 299 seconds
Leased Messages: 0
Executor queue size: 0
Callback queue size: 0
Request queue size: 167
```

The load is `1.67` because there are `167` requests which means the stream is *paused*. Because the stream is paused when the consumer tries to restart the stream the RPC is never started because to *start* the RPC the iterator must at least be started and the request iterator is blocked on the load going down. It won't go down because it *can't* send requests until the RPC is started.

issue_title: [PubSub Client Library][ML Engine v1.7][Python 3.5] Import Broken
issue_body:
When using the Python google-cloud-pubsub library within the Google Cloud ML Engine v1.7 runtime utilizing Python 3.5, attempting to import the library results in a module not found error. This ticket follows up on an issue opened with Google Cloud Support (Case#15774813) which has been passed along to the ML Engine team as far as I was told.

Notably, not all versions of the library are affected. Known broken versions include 0.32.1, 0.33.1, and 0.34.0. However, version 0.33.0 is known not to be broken. Additionally, it only appears to be this library affected as other third party libraries have no issue. Finally, this issue is not reproducible locally. Using Python version 3.5 in my local environment the below sample works without incident.

Traceback:
```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/lib/python3.5/runpy.py", line 184, in _run_module_as_main
    "__main__", mod_spec)
  File "/usr/lib/python3.5/runpy.py", line 85, in _run_code
    exec(code, run_globals)
  File "/root/.local/lib/python3.5/site-packages/trainer/task.py", line 1, in <module>
    import google.cloud.pubsub
ImportError: No module named 'google.cloud.pubsub'
```

To demo the problem, the following code can be used. To use, run the Makefile using `make LOCATION=gs://...` configuring the LOCATION variable to a Google Cloud Storage location you wish to upload the package to. You can also use `make clean LOCATION` to remove the packaging artifacts and the file from the LOCATION you specify.

./Makefile
```make
.POSIX:
.PHONY: help clean deps build test \
	run-local run-tune run-cloud run-deploy run-rollback

LOCATION = gs://YOUR_BUCKET_HERE/demo.tar.gz

run: TRAINING_ARGS  = demo_$(shell date +%Y_%m_%d__%H_%M_%S)
run: TRAINING_ARGS += --module-name trainer.task
run: TRAINING_ARGS += --package-path trainer
run: TRAINING_ARGS += --packages $(LOCATION)
run: TRAINING_ARGS += --staging-bucket $(shell dirname $(LOCATION))
run: TRAINING_ARGS += --region us-east1
run: TRAINING_ARGS += --config config.yaml
run: TRAINING_ARGS += --runtime-version 1.7
run: ./dist/demo-0.tar.gz
	gsutil cp ./dist/demo-0.tar.gz $(LOCATION)
	gcloud ml-engine jobs submit training $(TRAINING_ARGS)

clean:
	rm -rf dist/ *.egg-info
	-gsutil rm $(LOCATION) 2>/dev/null
	-gsutil rm -r $(shell dirname $(LOCATION))/demo_\* 2>/dev/null

./dist/demo-0.tar.gz: trainer/* setup.py
	python setup.py sdist
```

./config.yaml
```yaml
trainingInput:
  pythonVersion: "3.5"
```

./setup.py
```python
from setuptools import find_packages
from setuptools import setup

setup(
    name='demo',
    version='0',
    # Known broken versions: 0.34.0, 0.33.1, 0.32.1
    # Known working versions: 0.33.0
    install_requires=['google-cloud-pubsub==0.34.0'],
    packages=find_packages(),
    include_package_data=True,
    description='Demo showing broken import.',
)
```

./trainer/

./trainer/__init.py

./trainer/task.py
```python
import google.cloud.pubsub

print("Hello World")
```

issue_title: BigQuery: add argument to delete_dataset for deleteContents parameter
issue_labels: [{'id': 82126549, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/api:%20bigquery', 'name': 'api: bigquery', 'color': 'f8f8f8', 'default': False}, {'id': 544604243, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/priority:%20p2', 'name': 'priority: p2', 'color': 'fef2c0', 'default': False}, {'id': 77438582, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/type:%20feature%20request', 'name': 'type: feature request', 'color': 'c5def5', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
The [delete verb for datasets](https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/reference/rest/v2/datasets/delete) has a `deleteContents` parameter.

> If True, delete all the tables in the dataset. If False and the dataset contains tables, the request will fail. Default is False

We do not currently expose this parameter in [delete_dataset()](https://googlecloudplatform.github.io/google-cloud-python/latest/bigquery/reference.html#google.cloud.bigquery.client.Client.delete_dataset).

I could see this parameter being very handy in integration and snippets tests, where we want to delete a dataset and all the tables we have created.

issue_title: Low default chunk size slows GCS downloads
issue_labels: [{'id': 77678602, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/api:%20storage', 'name': 'api: storage', 'color': 'f8f8f8', 'default': False}, {'id': 461002860, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/performance', 'name': 'performance', 'color': 'bfdadc', 'default': False}, {'id': 544604243, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/priority:%20p2', 'name': 'priority: p2', 'color': 'fef2c0', 'default': False}, {'id': 744431537, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/type:%20process', 'name': 'type: process', 'color': 'c5def5', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
Steps to reproduce:
1. Create multi-gb file gs://your_bucket/obj
2. Test gsutil speed with "gsutil cp gs://your_bucket/obj /local/copy"
3. download via gcloud.storage.Blob.download_to_filename (see sample script below)

Expected result: similar timing

Actual result on my machine: gsutil downloads 4.6GB in 1min14sec, gcloud.storage takes 20min.

If I add `blob.chunk_size=1<<30` prior to download_to_filename, gcloud.storage takes the same time as gsutil to the second.

Sample script:

```
#!/usr/bin/env python
import httplib2
httplib2.debuglevel=4
import gcloud.storage

bucket_id = 'BUCKET'
blob_id = 'OBJECT'
url = 'gs://%s/%s' % (bucket_id, blob_id)
project = 'dnewgas-test-1'
client = gcloud.storage.client.Client(project)
bucket = client.get_bucket(bucket_id)
blob = bucket.get_blob(blob_id)
//blob.chunk_size=1<<30
blob.download_to_filename('/tmp/down')
```
